 For positions requiring access to controlled technology subject to export control regulations, including the U.S. Export Administration Regulations (EAR), ServiceNow may be required to obtain export control approval from government authorities for certain individuals. All employment is contingent upon ServiceNow obtaining any export license or other approval that may be required by relevant export control authorities.
   

  

 From Fortune. ©2025 Fortune Media IP Limited. All rights reserved. Used under license.

title: Aftersales Manager
description:
**The Company**
 We are Zeekr, built on a decade of European experience. We are a premium electric vehicle and technology solutions brand from Geely Holding Group, one of world’s largest automotive companies. Our goal and vision is to accelerate the shift to fully electrified mobility. We are committed to driving the future of transportation with cutting-edge technology and a customer-centric approach.
   

  

**The role**
 As Aftersales Lead you will build and grow our after sales business at ZEEKR NSC in Sweden. Built strong relationships with our customers by offering outstanding service and guidance through the chain and involvement into product and service development. The After Sales Lead is a vital part of the Leadership team in Sweden to steer to next level, reports to the Head of Country and works closely with the respective teams from Zeekr Europe located nearby in Amsterdam.
   

  

**This is what you will do:**
* Lead the After Sales Team in the NSC.
* Build and Strengthen Relationships: Foster and maintain strong relationships with the aftersales teams within our network across the country. Collaborate to ensure alignment with our after-sales service standards and customer satisfaction goals.
* Implement and monitor the implementation of the after-sales strategies developed and delivered by Zeekr Service Network Development, with the aim of improving customer satisfaction, customer experience, loyalty and retention. Encourage initiatives to improve service efficiency, streamline processes and reduce downtime for customers.
* Provide Training and Support: Conduct training sessions directly or through our partner for after sales teams on new products, technical updates, and service protocols. Offer continuous support and guidance, addressing technical inquiries and service-related challenges.
* Monitor Performance Metrics : Establish and monitor key performance indicators (KPIs) for after sales services or service training. Analyze data to identify trends, areas for improvement, and opportunities to enhance the overall after-sales experience.
* Resolve Escalated Issues: Act as a point of contact for escalated after-sales issues, working closely to resolve customer concerns promptly and effectively. Collaborate with internal teams to ensure swift resolution of technical and service-related challenges.
* Collaborate on Product Improvement: Gather feedback from after sales teams and customers to contribute to the continuous improvement of our products and services. Collaborate with cross-functional teams to implement product enhancements based on after-sales insights.
* Active Warranty Management follow up: Responsible for coordinating and overseeing first line warranty processes within the NSC. Monitor OEM warranty processes, service bulletins and the WPPM guidelines. Follow up warranty claim performance, accuracy, timelines. Coordinate action plans if required in the network.
* Active Technical support follow up: Responsible and ensure quick, accurate and professional support for technical issues, diagnostics and product related inquiries to maintain high service quality across the network.
* Active Parts & Logistics Management follow up: Responsible for coordinating, supporting and optimizing all first-line parts related processes within the NSC.
* Compliance: Adhering to regulatory requirements and industry standards.
* Quality and after-sales standard Assurance Lead: Ensure all dealership authorized service workshops operate in full compliance with the Zeekr quality, service and operational standards.


**To be successful in the role you will demonstrate:**
* Proven track record of successful after sales business development.
* Proven experiences on Dealer Management.
* Sufficient knowledge on P&L, Proven experiences in financial planning and execution.
* Proven experiences in After Sales operations management.
* Sufficient knowledge and understanding of customer experience, customer satisfaction and customer approach.
* Leading team with clear R&R, goals/objectives.
* Engaging & developing people, diving high-performance culture.
* Problem-solving, critical thinking and decision-making skills.
* Encouraging collaborations and fostering heathy work environment.
* Strong management level reporting and presentations.
* Extensive experience in after-sales management within the automotive industry, working for an OEM or experience gained by dealer holdings.
* Experienced in managing an OEM workshop, where, among others, vehicle preparation, including accessory installation, repairs, warranty services, and diagnostic work, contributes to operational efficiency and positive business outcome.
* The ability to steer the detail of today and anticipate on what is coming up next.
* Strong technical understanding of electric vehicles and automotive systems.
* Flexible and able to adapt quickly to changes.
* Proven experience in building relationships for a premium automotive brand.
* Entrepreneurial skills to always improve from within by fostering and nurturing ideas from the team.
* Excellent problem-solving skills and effective communication in native language and English.
* Ability to lead with trust and care, adapt to a fast-paced environment, and embrace diversity.
* Strong team player and able to lead the team in a constructive, positive way to be able to achieve the team objectives, and individual team member objectives.


**Please submit your CV in English**
 To thrive at Zeekr we believe that you have the courage to lead with trust and care and you don’t mind getting your hands dirty. You are highly innovative and have a great ability to adapt and perform in a constantly changing, fast-paced, highly demanding work environment. Like us, you believe it’s important to have a passion to work in an international and multicultural environment with the desire and ability to embrace the diversity of people, perspectives, and styles.
   

  

*Apply today, we will perform ongoing selection during the application period. We look forward to hearing from you.*
*Please note that due to GDPR regulations we can only accept applications sent through the recruitment system, not via email or other channels. We do not accept applications submitted by external recruitment companies.*

title: Senior Analytics Engineer
description:
Avalanche Studios Group is looking for a Senior Analytics Engineer to join our Data and Insights team. This role will be responsible for creating, designing and maintaining our data architecture. This role requires a deep understanding of data engineering, data modelling, data visualization, event design and best practices in those areas.
   

  

 This is a key role in developing our data lake by bridging the gap between our data engineer and our data scientists as well as ensuring that our data is timely, easy to work with and trusted across the company.
   

  

**What You'll Do**
* Develop and implement processes to extract, clean, and transform data from various sources to ensure it is ready for consumption/analysis.
* Collaborate with data scientists and analysts to understand their data needs and provide them with reliable datasets / data marts.
* Design and implement data models using industry best practices that capture a complete ecosystem view of in- and out-of-game experiences (major game analytics KPI’s, user profile/history) - while ensuring accuracy, compliance, scalability, and long-term usability.
* Create and maintain comprehensive data documentation, including data dictionaries, schemas, and metadata. Ensure that data definitions are consistent and easily accessible to all stakeholders.
* Monitor data pipelines and systems to ensure high availability and performance alongside our data engineer ; implement automated data quality checks, validation processes and alerts ensuring the accuracy and reliability of data.
* Write efficient, maintainable, and scalable code using best practices.
* Conduct code and SQL reviews and provide feedback to ensure high standards of code quality within the team.
* Stay updated with the latest trends, technologies and best practices in data engineering and analytics and share knowledge with the team.


**Who You Are**
* Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Computer Science, Data Engineering, or a related field ; with 5+ years experience as an Analytics Engineer, Data Engineer, Data Architect, or similar role.
* Strong proficiency in SQL and experience in designing efficient and scalable data models with large data sets across multiple teams.
* Experience with ETL/ELT processes and tools (e.g. Apache Airflow, Talend, DBT... DBT being a big plus).
* Familiarity with cloud-based data platforms (e.g. AWS, GCP, Azure… GCP being a big plus) ; and experience with “Infrastructure as Code” process (e.g. Terraform).
* Excellent problem-solving skills and attention to detail ; coupled with strong communication skills and the ability to work collaboratively with cross-functional teams.
* Passion for gaming and a deep understanding of the gaming industry is a plus!


 Note: Research shows under-represented groups in the industry might not apply to a job if they don’t check off every requirement. At Avalanche, we’re more about your drive to learn and interest in tackling challenges than what’s on your resume! So don’t stress if you don’t tick every box— shoot us your application anyway! Diverse opinions and different experiences are what make our teams so great, not itemized lists of qualifications.
   

  

**The Stockholm location**
 Our Stockholm office is situated in Södermalm, the beating heart of the city’s game industry. It’s a physical representation of our games – the lush vegetation of Just Cause and theHunter: Call of the Wild meets corroded metal straight out of Mad Max. Our hybrid model requires you to be in the office on Mondays, Tuesdays, and Thursdays to foster collaboration and creativity.
   

  

**Our values**
 At Avalanche Studios Group, we create worlds beyond limits. But that’s not exclusive to our games, so we’re committed to developing a diverse and inclusive workplace. All Avalanchers have a shared responsibility to create an open work environment where everyone is treated equally and respectfully. Being part of our world is not contingent on your heritage, your gender, or sexual orientation. It’s all about your passion and creativity.
   

  

**How To Apply**
 To apply for this position, please register below and provide your CV in English. We review applications continuously. All further studio-related information is provided under a non-disclosure agreement.
   

  

 At Avalanche Studios Group we're always on the lookout for talented individuals to join our passionate team, but please note that relocation assistance is not available for this role.

title: Warehouse Operator (fixed-term)
location: Upplands Väsby och Sollentuna, Stockholm County, Sweden
description:
**Want to shape the future of waterborne transport and join a world-class team?**
 Candela is the
 **global leader in electric hydrofoil vessels** 
 . Since 2014, we’ve gone from building our first premium electric speedboat in a small Stockholm workshop to delivering game-changing vessels around the globe.
 **The Candela P-12** 
 is our latest innovation - the world’s first electric hydrofoil ferry is not just operating in Stockholm, but now attracting orders from around the world.
   

  

 Our boats “fly” thanks to
 **computer-guided** 
 underwater wings under the hull made out of
 **lightweight carbon fiber.** 
 The wings lift the vessel above the water, which results in cutting energy use by up to 80%, while offering zero emissions and a far better passenger experience than traditional diesel vessels.
   

  

 And that’s exactly why we do this. Our mission is clear: to
 **speed up the transition to fossil fuel–free lakes and oceans** 
 .
   

  

 You can be part of the amazing team at Candela now! We are looking for a
 **Warehouse Operator** 
 to grow our warehouse team and support Candela’s future growth ambitions. This is a two-year fixed-term position.
   

  

**Your core responsibilities will include:**
**Goods Receiving, & Inventory Control**
* Receive, unload, load, and inspect incoming and outgoing shipments.
* Verify quantities and product conditions against purchase orders, sales orders, and delivery documentation.
* Report discrepancies, damages, or missing items in Odoo.
* Perform physical counts and maintain accurate inventory records.
* Support cycle counts and inventory audits.
* Investigate inventory discrepancies and support corrective actions.
* Maintain accurate inventory records and stock integrity.


**Warehouse Operations**
* Store materials and products in designated warehouse locations.
* Ensure proper labeling, storage, and handling of materials.
* Pick and deliver materials for manufacturing orders according to production schedules.
* Maintain warehouse organization, cleanliness, and efficiency.
* Support continuous improvement initiatives within warehouse operations.


**Health & Safety**
* Follow all company health, safety, and quality procedures.
* Report safety observations, incidents, and hazards through TIA.
* Ensure compliance with warehouse safety standards and material handling requirements.
* Contribute to a safe, clean, and organized working environment.


**What are we looking for?**
 We are looking for someone with a strong attention to detail and a clear commitment to accuracy. The ideal candidate is able to prioritize tasks effectively and perform well in a fast-paced environment. Strong communication skills and the ability to work collaboratively in a team are essential. Additionally, a positive attitude combined with a proactive approach to problem-solving is highly valued.
   

  

**Requirements:**
* One year of experience in warehouse, logistics, shipping, or supply chain operations.
* A forklift license.
* Be able to communicate in English as it is required in your daily work
* Experience working with ERP or Warehouse Management Systems (Odoo experience is highly desirable).
* Experience in a startup/scaleup company is a big plus.


**Form of employment** 
 : Full-time fixed-term position (two years).
   

  

**Start date:** 
 ASAP
   

  

**Location/base** 
 : Rotebro
   

  

**Team** 
 : Warehouse & Logistics
   

  

**Reporting to** 
 : Warehouse Manager
   

  

**Why people love working at Candela?**
 At Candela, we’re not just building electric vessels - we’re creating a whole new category of clean, efficient waterborne transport. You get the chance to work with
 **cutting-edge technology** 
 , solving complex challenges, and seeing the results in action - sometimes literally, when we organize test rides for our newcomers! We’re a diverse and ambitious team of over 50 nationalities, united by a
 **revolutionary spirit and kindness** 
 . That means we work hard, but always with humility and while having fun - whether it’s solving a manufacturing challenge, improving processes, or cheering each other on during
 **Candela Months** 
 , our year-round calendar of bonding activities and events.
   

  

**Recruitment process:**
 We aim to keep our recruitment process as straightforward and efficient as possible. As an international company with English as our corporate language, the entire process is conducted in English. Therefore, please submit your application in our corporate language as well. Kindly note that we cannot process applications sent via email due to GDPR so please use our career website for you application.
   

  

**We welcome the opportunity to meet you!**

title: Treasury Lead
description:
**TL;DR:**
 You will build Lovable's treasury function from scratch, working directly with our Head of Tax. This is a high-ownership, high-impact role for someone who knows how to set up treasury infrastructure in a fast-moving environment and wants to do it at one of the fastest-growing AI companies in the world.
   

  

**Why Lovable?**
 Lovable lets anyone and everyone build software with any language. From solopreneurs to Fortune 100 teams, millions of people use Lovable to transform raw ideas into real products - fast. We are at the forefront of a foundational shift in software creation, which means you have an unprecedented opportunity to change the way the digital world works. Lovable-built applications and websites are visited hundreds of millions of times a month, and our enterprise footprint is compounding fast. And we’re just getting started.
   

  

 We’re a small, talent-dense team building a generation-defining company from Stockholm. We value extreme ownership, high velocity, and low-ego collaboration. We seek out people who care deeply, ship fast, and are eager to make a dent in the world.
   

  

**What We’re Looking For**
 Lovable is scaling fast across multiple entities and geographies. As our first Treasury Lead, you'll work alongside the Head of Tax to build the treasury function from the ground up: investment strategy, cash management, FX framework, banking relationships, and intercompany structure.
   

  

 This is not a process-maintenance role. You'll be building, not inheriting, across a multi-entity, multi-currency business that is growing quickly. If you thrive in greenfield environments, find smarter ways to work, and want real ownership from day one, this is for you.
   

  

**You should have:**
* Minimum 8+ years of treasury experience, ideally spanning cash management, investments, FX, and banking
* Experience setting up or significantly building out a treasury function, not just running one
* Strong understanding of cash investment strategies and risk management frameworks
* Hands-on experience with FX risk management and hedging in a multi-currency, multi-entity environment
* Background in high-growth tech or SaaS - you understand the pace and the lack of playbook
* Experience building and maintaining banking relationships and driving cost reduction across fees and transactions
* A genuine interest in AI tools and agentic ways of working - you look for smarter approaches, not just faster ones
* The ability to work independently and make decisions without a team behind you yet
* Excellent communication skills in English
* Based in Stockholm or willing to relocate


**What You’ll Do**
* Build and own Lovable's investment policy and cash management framework across all entities
* Optimise yield on cash balances while maintaining appropriate liquidity and risk parameters
* Establish treasury infrastructure to support the business as it scales across multiple geographies and entities
* Build and implement an FX framework as international revenue grows
* Drive cost reduction across FX, banking fees, and payment transactions
* Build up and maintain banking relationships across the group
* Own daily cash visibility across all entities and banks
* Manage intercompany financing arrangements and ensure they are properly documented
* Partner with Finance, Tax, and Legal on treasury implications of commercial and structural decisions
* Build scalable treasury processes and actively explore how AI and automation can make the function smarter


**About Your Application**
 Please submit your application in English. It’s our company language, so you’ll be speaking lots of it if you join.
   

  

 We treat all candidates equally - if you’re interested, please apply through our careers portal.

title: Product Lifecycle Manager
description:
Are you passionate about bringing structure, clarity, and customer focus to complex product portfolios? ASSA ABLOY Global Solutions is looking for a Product Lifecycle Manager to help shape and orchestrate the future of our Hospitality Access products.
   

  

 Reporting to the Senior Director & Head of Access Product Management, you will sit at the heart of our Access product portfolio, working across Product Management, Marketing, Sales, Engineering, and regional teams to ensure that products are launched, evolved, and retired in a coordinated, customer centric way. You will play a key role in turning fragmented roadmaps into cohesive portfolio journeys that deliver clear value to customers and the business.
   

  

 This is a unique opportunity to join a global, technology driven organization where products are complex, stakeholders are diverse, and influence matters more than formal authority. In this role, you’ll thrive in a culture that values engineering excellence, ownership, and continuous learning, empowering you to build a career you can be proud of.
   

  

 Flexibility for the right candidate to be located in Sweden (preferred), Norway, or the UK, with travel expected as part of this global role.
   

  

**What You Will Do As Our Product Lifecycle Manager**
 As a Product Lifecycle Manager, you will orchestrate lifecycle activities across the Hospitality Access portfolio, ensuring alignment, transparency, and customer focus from idea to end of life. You will enable Product Managers and regional stakeholders to execute launches, migrations, and retirements in a structured and commercially sound way.
   

  

 You would also:
   

  

* Work closely with Product Managers to lead cross functional alignment across product families, ensuring launches, migrations, and product retirements are coordinated, coherent, and managed at portfolio level.
* Track lifecycle milestones end to end, proactively highlighting risks and facilitating discussions to address gaps in commercial value, customer impact, execution readiness, and upgrade or phase out paths.
* Partner with Product Management, Product Marketing, Sales, and regional teams to orchestrate go to market activities, ensuring products are delivered with clear customer value, timing, and organizational readiness.
* Coordinate the development and alignment of messaging, training, and launch materials, translating complex technical content into clear, customer focused value propositions.
* Act as the central liaison for regional feedback by establishing structured engagement forums, consolidating insights, and sharing clear, actionable reporting with relevant stakeholders.
* Design, implement, and continuously improve lifecycle management frameworks, tools, and ways of working to increase portfolio visibility, decision making quality, and execution efficiency.
* Support portfolio governance by developing robust business cases (market data, regional and R&D inputs, investment logic) and monitoring compliance with security, quality, and regulatory requirements, while applying best practices in lifecycle and project management within a global matrix organization.
* Collaborate closely across teams as the role evolves, taking on additional responsibilities and adapting priorities in line with business needs in a dynamic, growing environment.


**The Skills And Experience You Need**
 We are looking for someone who has:
   

  

* 4+ years of experience in product lifecycle management, project or program coordination, or a similar operational role within a technology driven or B2B environment, with exposure to launches, migrations, phase outs, or pilot programs.
* Experience working with both hardware and software products, ideally within complex, long lifecycle, or integrated systems.
* Proven ability to operate effectively in matrix organizations, influencing without formal authority and aligning diverse, cross functional, and regional stakeholders.
* Strong organizational, analytical, and problem solving skills, with the ability to structure ambiguity, assess opportunities, and drive progress.
* Excellent communication and stakeholder management skills, comfortable engaging with senior leaders, regional teams, and cross functional partners; fluent in English, both written and spoken.
* Experience supporting business case development, including market sizing, opportunity framing, and coordination of inputs, with familiarity with tools such as Jira, Confluence, or similar collaboration and planning tools considered a plus.


 The ideal candidate has a strong commercial mindset, curiosity, and a genuine interest in understanding customer value. They are comfortable switching between operational delivery and higher level portfolio thinking, enjoy working across functions and regions, and thrive in environments where priorities evolve—especially during intensive phases such as pilot programs and product launches.
   

  

 We value attitude as much as experience. If you are adaptable, proactive, and energized by bringing order and clarity to complex product ecosystems—while growing and evolving with the business—this role offers a strong opportunity to make a visible impact and build a meaningful career.
   

  

**What We Offer**
 We’re passionate about providing amazing opportunities and benefits, so you can continue and progress a lifelong career with us – here’s what we have to offer:
   

  

* Learning and career development opportunities, whether it’s online learning, management training or enhancing your skills.
* A competitive salary
* Hybrid model of working
* Stable employment in a friendly international atmosphere


**We review applications regularly, so don't wait.**
 We are building diverse and inclusive teams and encourage applications from all who can envision themselves working with us.
   

  

 To ensure that your personal information is secure, we do not review any applications sent via email or post. If you have any questions about the role or the process, please send an email to Khalil Kabakibi, Talent Acquisition Business Partner, at Khalil.kabakibi@assaabloy.com .
   

  

 Let's together create a safer and more open world!
   

  

 To find out more about us, visit www.assaabloy.com.
   

  

**We are the ASSA ABLOY Group**
 Our people have made us the global leader in access solutions. In return, we open doors for them wherever they go. With nearly 63,000 colleagues in more than 70 different countries, we help billions of people experience a more open world. Our innovations make all sorts of spaces – physical and virtual – safer, more secure, and easier to access.
   

  

 As an employer, we value results – not titles, or backgrounds. We empower our people to build their career around their aspirations and our ambitions – supporting them with regular feedback, training, and development opportunities. Our colleagues think broadly about where they can make the most impact, and we encourage them to grow their role locally, regionally, or even internationally.
   

  

 As we welcome new people on board, it’s important to us to have diverse, inclusive teams, and we value different perspectives and experiences.

title: Principal Platform Engineer
description:
**Mission**
 Neko is redefining what prevention means, from treating illness when it arrives, to sustaining health before it's ever at risk. Our mission: make data-driven, preventative care accessible to more people, before symptoms appear.
   

  

 In a single, non-invasive visit under an hour, proprietary technology and direct clinical care combine to deliver personalised, actionable insights. It's a team that thinks in 10x, not 10%. Every role here plays a part in building a world where prevention is the norm, and where your work genuinely helps people live longer, healthier lives.
   

  

**The Role**
 This is a 0→1 build. You'll own the architecture, build and rollout of Neko's internal AI developer platform, shaping how our engineers design, ship and operate software through agentic, AI-assisted workflows.
   

  

 It's a hands-on technical leadership role. You'll be the person setting long-term direction for AI agents, tooling and the systems underneath them, while also doing the work. The expectation is that what you build gets used across teams and meaningfully changes how engineering happens here.
   

  

**What You'll Deliver**
*In the first 6 months*
* A clear architectural direction for the platform
* Initial components shipped and adopted by early teams
* Measurable improvements to developer speed, reliability and autonomy
* Genuine organic adoption across engineering, driven by value not mandate


*By month 12*
* A substantive shift toward agentic, engineer-led development workflows
* Platform scaled beyond pilot groups to multiple teams
* Clear ownership models for agents, tooling and supporting systems
* Long-term architecture defined and moving toward maturity


**What We're Looking For**
*Must-haves*
* Proven experience building and shipping an internal developer platform, hands-on, not advisory
* A strong track record leading agentic or AI-assisted development work that engineers actually use day-to-day
* Real backend engineering depth with production systems, recent Java or C# strongly preferred
* Experience rolling out platform tooling to 20-30+ developers with meaningful adoption
* Expertise with workflow engines such as AWS Step Functions, Azure Durable Functions, Temporal, Conductor or Dagster
* The ability to drive change through influence and credibility, not positional authority
* 10-15+ years of engineering experience with substantial platform ownership


*Nice to have*
* Full-stack exposure, though backend depth is the priority
* Background in product-led SaaS or organisations with strong internal developer tooling culture


**About Titles At Neko**
 We use a simplified internal title framework that prioritises clarity over hierarchy, so internal titles may differ from market‑facing role titles. Scope, impact and level of the role are fully aligned and will be clearly discussed throughout the process.
   

  

**Hiring Process**
 Candidates progress from application and structured screening through thoughtfully designed interviews culminating in a formal offer and final pre-employment checks before joining the team.
   

  

**Equal Opportunity & Inclusion Statement**
 Neko Health is committed to inclusive hiring and member-first care. We welcome candidates from all backgrounds and encourage you to request reasonable adjustments to support your application.

title: ADMS/Scada-consultants
description:
**Job Summary**
 We are seeking highly skilled and visionary OT (Operational Technology) Solutions Architects and Consultants to lead and conduct design and implementation of cutting-edge Smart Grid and utility automation solutions. These roles demand a deep understanding of OT systems, grid modernization technologies, and enterprise integration strategies. The ideal candidates will possess hands-on experience with GE GridOS software, GE ADMS / Scada Series 6, Scada PowerOn and GridOS Connect & Fabric and will be responsible for architecting and implementing scalable, secure, and future-ready solutions that support our clients’ digital transformation initiatives in the energy and utilities sector.
   

  

 The candidates will collaborate with cross-functional teams to translate business requirements into technical architectures, ensuring alignment with industry standards and regulatory frameworks. Your work will directly impact the reliability, efficiency, and sustainability of critical infrastructure systems.
   

  

 The role can be located in either Stockholm or Göteborg.
   

  

**Key Responsibilities Solution Architects / Lead Engineers / Scada Migration Consultants**
 The responsibilities will span the full stack of Grid Modernization programs:
   

  

* Design and deliver comprehensive OT architecture solutions tailored to Smart Grid environments and utility operations.
* Conduct/lead integration and deployment of GE GridOS software, GE ADMS / Scada Series 6 & Power On, GridOS Connect & Fabric platforms across diverse client landscapes.
* Develop and implement reference architectures and solution blueprints that support distributed energy resources (DER), grid automation, and real-time monitoring.
* Collaborate with IT and cybersecurity teams to ensure secure data flows between OT and IT domains.
* Conduct technical assessments, gap analyses, and feasibility studies to support strategic planning and investment decisions.
* Provide technical leadership throughout the solution lifecycle—from concept and design to implementation and support.
* Engage with vendors, partners, and clients to evaluate emerging technologies and recommend enhancements to existing systems.
* Implement new gird-solutions based on the GE Smart Grid software
* Ensure compliance with industry standards such as IEC 61850, NERC CIP, and ISO 27001.
* Mentor junior engineers and contribute to knowledge-sharing initiatives across the organization.


**Required Skills & Qualifications**
* Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Electrical Engineering, Computer Science, or a related field.
* Proven experience in OT architecture, Smart Grid technologies, and utility automation.
* Strong expertise in GE GridOS software, GE ADMS / Scada Series 6 & PowerOn or GridOS Connect & Fabric.
* In-depth knowledge of SCADA systems, substation automation, and real-time data acquisition.
* Familiarity with network protocols (DNP3, Modbus, IEC 61850) and industrial communication standards.
* Ability to design secure and resilient architectures that bridge OT and IT environments.
* Excellent problem-solving, communication, and stakeholder management skills.
* Experience with cloud platforms, edge computing, and IoT integration is a plus.


**Additional Skills**
* Experience working with Smart Grid analytics, predictive maintenance, and AI-driven optimization.
* Knowledge of digital twin technologies and simulation tools for grid operations.
* Certification in TOGAF, CISSP, or similar frameworks.
* Prior involvement in large-scale utility transformation programs or grid modernization projects.


**What Makes These Roles Stand Out**
 This isn’t just another architecture or consultant role—it’s a chance to lead the transformation of utility operations using advanced Smart Grid platforms like GE GridOS and GE ADMS / Scada. You’ll be part of the technical backbone of projects that modernize infrastructure, improve grid reliability, and enable sustainable energy solutions.

title: Agentic ERP Solution Engineer (NetSuite)
description:
**Join our global force of 400+ innovators, blending the latest in tech with the greatest in soundtracking, from our Stockholm HQ to offices in London, New York, Los Angeles, Berlin, Paris, Oslo, and Seoul. We’re an industry leader with a startup mentality. We take what we do seriously, but we don’t take ourselves too seriously. Creating and collaborating to transform the sound of streaming, content, and culture. Come join us, and let the world** ***feel*** **your work.**

As a Agentic ERP Solution Engineering, you will be a core member of the Financial Systems team that owns the systems running Epidemic Sound's financial backbone. Your role is to translate complex financial and operational business needs into scalable technical solutions, working across our ERP ecosystem with a focus on process design, solution architecture, and agentic ways of working.


You will work at the intersection of Tech and Finance, where your ability to understand both business intent and technical possibility directly affects how reliably we run financial operations and scale as a company. You will collaborate closely with engineers across the Tech organisation, the Finance team, and various stakeholders across the organization to understand business needs, streamline processes, and leverage the full capabilities of Netsuite to support our long-term growth.

**Your key responsibilities include**
-------------------------------------

* Translate complex Finance, Procurement, and operational business requirements into scalable, end-to-end ERP solutions, from discovery through to deployment.
* Design, build, and maintain robust solutions within our ERP tech stack (centered on NetSuite), including integrations with connected systems and external platforms.
* Design and implement agentic workflows and AI-driven automation to streamline financial processes, identifying and evaluating new tools to drive efficiency and accuracy.
* Proactively surface process inefficiencies and data integrity issues through AI-assisted analysis, continuously optimizing for long-term scalability.
* Act as a technical subject matter expert, communicating directly with stakeholders to translate business intent into technical execution and driving cross-functional initiatives.

**Requirements**
----------------

* Solid experience working with ERP platforms, including strong NetSuite configuration and integrations, alongside deep understanding of financial processes like procure-to-pay and order-to-cash.
* Proven ability to translate ambiguous business requirements into clear, scalable, and high-impact technical solutions across complex environments.
* Genuine curiosity about and practical experience applying AI and agentic tools in a business context, balancing automation leverage with human judgment.
* Strong communication and facilitation skills, with the ability to partner directly with Finance and Tech teams to drive initiatives while operating as a senior individual contributor.
* Act as a technical subject matter expert, partnering with stakeholders and engineering teams to solve complex problems, improve data integrity, and drive cross-functional initiatives.
* trong project leadership and stakeholder communication, with a track record of driving initiatives from discovery to measurable business impact and translating between business intent and technical execution.

**It would also be music to our ears if you have**
--------------------------------------------------

* Familiarity with invoice-to-pay, spend management, consolidation, or planning and budgeting software such as Medius, Expensify, Aaro, or NSPB.
* Experience working across ERP and CRM ecosystems, including Salesforce integrations, paired with knowledge of modern DevOps practices and agile ways of working.

**Equal opportunity employer**


We believe that bringing people together from different backgrounds, experiences and perspectives makes for a healthy workplace, a more successful business and a better world. We value diversity and encourage everyone to come and soundtrack the world with us.

 **Application**


Ready to make the world *feel* your work? Please apply, in English.

title: Senior DevOps Engineer
description:
Vi bygger framtidens restaurangplattformar – vill du vara med?
  
ChopChop söker en Senior DevOps Engineer till vårt team i Rosersberg!
  
Är du en erfaren DevOps Engineer som brinner för stabila system, smart automation och modern infrastruktur? Trivs du i en miljö där du får stort ansvar och möjlighet att påverka? Då kanske det är dig vi söker!
  
Vi letar efter en senior DevOps Engineer som vill vara med och utveckla och förvalta den tekniska plattform som driver ChopChops digitala verksamhet framåt.
  
Vad innebär rollen hos oss?
  
Som DevOps Engineer hos oss arbetar du från vårt huvudkontor Gigachop i Rosersberg tillsammans med vårt utvecklingsteam som idag består av fyra personer. Du blir en nyckelperson i arbetet med att säkerställa att våra system är stabila, säkra och skalbara.
  
  

**Du kommer att:**  

Ansvara för och vidareutveckla vår molninfrastruktur och plattform
  
  

Arbeta med AWS, Kubernetes och moderna DevOps-verktyg
  
  

Utveckla och förbättra våra CI/CD-processer
  
  

Säkerställa hög tillgänglighet, säkerhet och prestanda i våra system
  
  

Arbeta med övervakning, logghantering och incidenthantering
  
  

Automatisera processer och effektivisera vår utvecklingsmiljö
  
  

Samarbeta nära utvecklarna för att skapa robusta och driftsäkra lösningar
  
  

Här får du möjlighet att ta stort tekniskt ansvar, påverka arkitektur och arbetssätt samt vara med och bygga en plattform som används i hela verksamheten.
  
Vad kan du förvänta dig av oss?
  
Ett litet och kompetent utvecklingsteam med korta beslutsvägar
  
  

En nyckelroll där du får stort inflytande över teknik och infrastruktur
  
  

Möjlighet att arbeta nära verksamheten och se resultatet av ditt arbete
  
  

Ett företag i stark tillväxt med höga ambitioner
  
  

Ett modernt huvudkontor i Rosersberg där vi arbetar tillsammans på plats
  
  

Frihet under ansvar och möjlighet att utvecklas tillsammans med bolaget
  
  

**Vi tror att du:**  

Har flera års erfarenhet av DevOps, Cloud eller Platform Engineering
  
  

Har gedigen erfarenhet av AWS och Kubernetes
  
  

Har arbetat med CI/CD, Infrastructure as Code och automatisering
  
  

Har erfarenhet av övervakning, loggning och incidenthantering
  
  

Är van att ta ägarskap och driva tekniska förbättringar
  
  

Gillar att arbeta nära utvecklare och verksamheten
  
  

Är lösningsorienterad, strukturerad och självgående
  
  

Tjänsten är placerad på vårt huvudkontor Gigachop i Rosersberg och kräver närvaro på plats.
  
**Lön:** Månadslön enligt överenskommelse.

title: Staff Platform Engineer
description:
* ️ What We Do


Flower is Flexible Power. We are a next-gen energy company leveraging AI and machine learning to make renewable energy stable and always available – even when the sun isn’t shining and the wind isn’t blowing.
  
Through smart optimization and trading of energy assets like wind and solar farms, battery systems, and EV chargers, we make renewable energy reliable and predictable, leading the charge towards the energy system of tomorrow.
  
  

Who We Are
  
Tech company at heart – purpose in our DNA. Flower consists of a diverse group of innovative individuals with a strong desire to improve the state of the world.
  
At Flower, we believe trust, collaboration and diversity are essential to not only create an inclusive work environment, but also drive career growth. By embracing varying perspectives, we allow creativity and progress to flourish.
  
To accelerate towards our goal of becoming the pioneering force powering the energy system of tomorrow, we are now looking for a passionate and skilled Staff Platform Engineer to join our Platform team.
  
  

About the role
  
Flower runs renewable energy assets at the edge of what's technically possible. Our traders make decisions in seconds, our IoT devices stream telemetry from sites across Europe, and our data and AI systems sit in the middle of it all. The platform that enables these workloads has to be fast, reliable, and a joy to build on.
  
That's where you come in.
  
As Staff Platform Engineer you will set the technical direction for how Flower's platform evolves, with a particular focus on DevOps, SRE, and developer experience. You'll work alongside the Head of Platform and a team of platform and data engineers, and your customers are the product squads building Flower's trading and AI systems. Your job is to make them faster, safer, and more productive.
  
This is a hands-on technical leadership role. You'll write code, design systems, mentor engineers, and shape practice across the group, without formal management responsibility.
  
  

What you'll do
  
  

Set technical direction across the platform engineering group, raising the bar on architecture, code quality, and operational excellence.
  
  

Drive Flower's DevOps practice and scale it across all product workloads, including CI/CD on GitHub Actions, deployment patterns, and release safety.
  
  

Treat the platform as a product. Understand the pain points of our trading, AI, and product squads firsthand, and prioritise platform investments accordingly.
  
  

Own and evolve ECS & EKS on AWS as a first-class workload platform, including service templates, autoscaling, networking, and developer tooling.
  
  

Own and evolve Modal as a core compute substrate for batch jobs, agents, and trading-adjacent workloads.
  
  

Partner with our Platform Engineers to stand up Flower's SRE practice, including on-call, incident response, SLOs, and operational readiness.
  
  

Support observability work on Grafana Cloud alongside the engineers who own that domain today.
  
  

Mentor platform engineers across the group / squads.
  
  

Who you are
  
  

You have 8+ years of engineering experience, with meaningful time spent as a backend or product engineer before moving into platform or DevOps work. You know what a bad platform feels like from the inside, and you care about fixing it.
  
  

You have led technical work across a group of engineers, whether as a tech lead, staff engineer, or principal. You set direction without needing a manager title to do it.
  
  

You have strong, hands-on experience operating production workloads on AWS, particularly ECS or EKS. You're comfortable with task and service design, autoscaling, deployment strategies, and networking.
  
  

You have practical experience with managed or serverless compute platforms such as Modal, Lambda, or Cloud Run.
  
  

**You have deep DevOps fundamentals:** CI/CD design (GitHub Actions or similar), release safety, progressive delivery, environment strategy, secrets handling.
  
  

**You have solid SRE fundamentals:** on-call design, incident response, SLOs and error budgets, postmortem culture.
  
  

You're comfortable with infrastructure as code (Terraform), Linux, and networking fundamentals.
  
  

You're familiar with observability stacks (Grafana Cloud, OTel, or similar) and can partner credibly with the engineer who owns that domain.
  
  

You've worked in a scale-up or growth-stage engineering org and you're comfortable with ambiguity, fast iteration, and small high-trust teams.
  
  

You're collaborative, low-ego, and you bring others up around you.
  
  

Location
  
Our beautiful office is located in the heart of Södermalm, just a short walk from Slussen subway station. We encourage in-office collaboration but support a hybrid work model.
  
  

✉️ Apply
  
Our corporate language is English, as we have about 30 nationalities in the office. We therefore appreciate it if you could submit your CV in English.
  
Throughout the recruitment process you will meet with the Talent Partner, the Head of Platform, our Product Manager, the Team for a live-coding and system design case, and the VP of Engineering.
  
We look forward to hearing from you!
  
—-
  
We kindly but firmly decline any engagement in recruitment assistance for our hiring processes. This includes partnership offers or the sale of recruitment tools.

title: DevOps Engineer/Architect
description:
Jobbeskrivning


Som DevOps Engineer och konsult hos oss blir du en viktig del av vårt Tech-team, där samarbete, kunskapsdelning och utveckling står i fokus. Du kommer att stötta både nya och befintliga kunder i att bygga, vidareutveckla och förvalta moderna infrastrukturlösningar anpassade efter deras behov.


Vi söker en erfaren DevOps-ingenjör som vill bidra till utvecklingen och förvaltningen av moderna IT-plattformar inom samhällsviktig verksamhet. Du kommer att arbeta i gränslandet mellan utveckling, drift och infrastruktur med fokus på automatisering, stabilitet och säkerhet.


I rollen ansvarar du för att utveckla, automatisera och förvalta plattformar och tjänster som stödjer verksamhetskritiska system. Du arbetar nära utvecklare, arkitekter, infrastrukturspecialister och verksamheten för att skapa effektiva leveransflöden och säkerställa hög tillgänglighet.


Kvalifikationer


Vi söker någon med kunskaper och erfarenheter enligt nedan, men vi vill samtidigt betona att du kan vara en intressant kandidat även om du inte uppfyller alla kriterier.

* Flytande svenska i tal och skrift
* Minst fem års arbetslivserfarenhet av DevOps i **offentlig sektor eller inom energisektorn**
* Erfarenhet med andra publika molnplattformar som Azure, AWS eller/och GCP
* Erfarenhet av containers och molntekniker (t.ex. Docker, Kubernetes, AWS, Azure)
* Flera års praktisk erfarenhet av Linux-hantering och skriptning och automation.
* God förståelse för DNS, routing, lastbalansering, nätverk och brandväggsteknik
* Några års praktisk erfarenhet av Azure DevOps (infra), GitLab, Github eller relaterade tjänster.
* Erfarenhet av att designa och utveckla CI/CD-miljöer samt implementera och hantera CI/CD-pipelines
* Erfarenhet av felsökning, t.ex. brandväggsinställningar, serverproblem, trafik mellan servrar
* Arkitekturkunskap om frontend-teknologier, backend-teknologier samt databaser och meddelandelösningar


Vi lägger även stor vikt vid dina personliga egenskaper som vi tror bland annat kännetecknas av att du är en samarbetsorienterad person som tycker om att ta ansvar, kan arbeta självständigt och har ett öga för detaljer.


Företagsbeskrivning


AFRY erbjuder tjänster inom teknik, design, digitalisering och rådgivning. Vi är dedikerade experter inom industri, energi och infrastruktur och skapar värde för kommande generationer. AFRY har nordiska rötter med global räckvidd. Våra gemensamma principer – Result driven, Client centric, Empowering och Accountable – vägleder oss i hur vi arbetar, utvecklas och lyckas tillsammans. Var med och accelerera övergången till ett hållbart samhälle.


Ytterligare information


Vår enhet Digital Services verkar över hela utvecklingskedjan med experter inom projektledning, kravanalys, QA och test, systemutveckling, arkitektur, dataanalys och AI, cybersäkerhet med mera.


Vad erbjuder AFRY dig som anställd?


Vi har regelbundna träffar där temat varierar mellan kompetens och socialt utbyte, eller både och. En bra balans helt enkelt! Hos oss får du också:

* En AFRY Buddy som bollplank under din första tid som anställd
* Balans i arbetslivet med möjlighet till hybridarbete.
* Tillgång till Club AFRY där en bredd av spännande och underhållande aktiviteter för våra medarbetare anordnas - det finns något för alla! Läs gärna mer här.

* Företagsrabatter med externa partners genom vår förmånsportal Advantage You.
* Friskvårdsbidrag 4500 SEK/år.
* Restauranger och kaféer på plats
* Ett enormt nätverk av tekniska experter med +19 000 kollegor runtom i världen.

*Vänligen notera att återkoppling på ansökningar kan dröja något med anledning av semestertider och beräknas ske först i augusti.*  

  

**Kontaktperson för frågor:**  

Aamir Faried, Gruppchef  

aamir.faried@afry.com  

  

Vi undanber oss direktkontakt med bemannings- och rekryteringsföretag samt säljare av ytterligare jobbannonser.

title: Staff Platform Engineer
description:
️ **What We Do**


Flower is Flexible Power. We are a next-gen energy company leveraging AI and machine learning to make renewable energy stable and always available – even when the sun isn’t shining and the wind isn’t blowing.


Through smart optimization and trading of energy assets like wind and solar farms, battery systems, and EV chargers, we make renewable energy reliable and predictable, leading the charge towards the energy system of tomorrow.


**Who We Are**


Tech company at heart – purpose in our DNA. Flower consists of a diverse group of innovative individuals with a strong desire to improve the state of the world.


At Flower, we believe trust, collaboration and diversity are essential to not only create an inclusive work environment, but also drive career growth. By embracing varying perspectives, we allow creativity and progress to flourish.


To accelerate towards our goal of becoming the pioneering force powering the energy system of tomorrow, we are now looking for a passionate and skilled Staff Platform Engineer to join our Platform team.

**About the role**


Flower runs renewable energy assets at the edge of what's technically possible. Our traders make decisions in seconds, our IoT devices stream telemetry from sites across Europe, and our data and AI systems sit in the middle of it all. The platform that enables these workloads has to be fast, reliable, and a joy to build on.


That's where you come in.


As Staff Platform Engineer you will set the technical direction for how Flower's platform evolves, with a particular focus on DevOps, SRE, and developer experience. You'll work alongside the Head of Platform and a team of platform and data engineers, and your customers are the product squads building Flower's trading and AI systems. Your job is to make them faster, safer, and more productive.


This is a hands-on technical leadership role. You'll write code, design systems, mentor engineers, and shape practice across the group, without formal management responsibility.

**What you'll do**

* Set technical direction across the platform engineering group, raising the bar on architecture, code quality, and operational excellence.
* Drive Flower's DevOps practice and scale it across all product workloads, including CI/CD on GitHub Actions, deployment patterns, and release safety.
* Treat the platform as a product. Understand the pain points of our trading, AI, and product squads firsthand, and prioritise platform investments accordingly.
* Own and evolve ECS & EKS on AWS as a first-class workload platform, including service templates, autoscaling, networking, and developer tooling.
* Own and evolve Modal as a core compute substrate for batch jobs, agents, and trading-adjacent workloads.
* Partner with our Platform Engineers to stand up Flower's SRE practice, including on-call, incident response, SLOs, and operational readiness.
* Support observability work on Grafana Cloud alongside the engineers who own that domain today.
* Mentor platform engineers across the group / squads.

**Who you are**

* You have 8+ years of engineering experience, with meaningful time spent as a backend or product engineer before moving into platform or DevOps work. You know what a bad platform feels like from the inside, and you care about fixing it.
* You have led technical work across a group of engineers, whether as a tech lead, staff engineer, or principal. You set direction without needing a manager title to do it.
* You have strong, hands-on experience operating production workloads on AWS, particularly ECS or EKS. You're comfortable with task and service design, autoscaling, deployment strategies, and networking.
* You have practical experience with managed or serverless compute platforms such as Modal, Lambda, or Cloud Run.
* You have deep DevOps fundamentals: CI/CD design (GitHub Actions or similar), release safety, progressive delivery, environment strategy, secrets handling.
* You have solid SRE fundamentals: on-call design, incident response, SLOs and error budgets, postmortem culture.
* You're comfortable with infrastructure as code (Terraform), Linux, and networking fundamentals.
* You're familiar with observability stacks (Grafana Cloud, OTel, or similar) and can partner credibly with the engineer who owns that domain.
* You've worked in a scale-up or growth-stage engineering org and you're comfortable with ambiguity, fast iteration, and small high-trust teams.
* You're collaborative, low-ego, and you bring others up around you.


**Location**


Our beautiful office is located in the heart of Södermalm, just a short walk from Slussen subway station. We encourage in-office collaboration but support a hybrid work model.


️ **Apply**


Our corporate language is English, as we have about 30 nationalities in the office. We therefore appreciate it if you could submit your CV in English.


Throughout the recruitment process you will meet with the Talent Partner, the Head of Platform, our Product Manager, the Team for a live-coding and system design case, and the VP of Engineering.


We look forward to hearing from you!


—-

*We kindly but firmly decline any engagement in recruitment assistance for our hiring processes. This includes partnership offers or the sale of recruitment tools.*

title: Funktionel Konsulent, Microsoft D365 CE – Kemp & Lauritzen, Albertslund
description:
**Funktionel Konsulent, Microsoft D365 CE – Kemp & Lauritzen, Albertslund**  

  

Vil du arbejde med det nyeste af det nye inden for D365CE og videreudvikle eksisterende og implementere ny funktionalitet på platformen samt sikre, at løsninger samkøres med en foranstående stor D365FO implementering? Du får mulighed for at arbejde bredt og dybt med D365 Sales, Customer Service, Field Service Management, kvalitetssikring og dokumentation og gøre det i et miljø med den nyeste teknologi, og som prioriterer ’Best of breed’, ’Microsoft first’ og ’Cloud first’.


Ikke alene er Kemp & Lauritzen en stolt dansk virksomhed fra 1800-tallet, med knap 3.000 ansatte og ejet af en fond med det formål at give tilbage til samfundet… De er også på en ambitiøs og vild digital rejse, hvor de er godt i gang med at opdatere hele applikationslandskabet – på tværs af hele Microsoft-paletten, dvs. FO, CE Sales og Field Service og Power-platformen i kombination med Power BI og Fabric.


Det stærke digitaliseringsfokus viser sig bl.a. ved, at Kemp & Lauritzen har skabt en innovativ kundeplatform, hvor Field Service spiller en stor rolle ifm. data – og det har også udmøntet sig i stor anerkendelse ved årets Danish Digital Awards, hvor Kemp & Lauritzen fik priser i seks kategorier: Guld i Use of AI, Guld i Digital Business Solutions, Guld i Use of Data, Sølv i Digital Business Transformation, Bronze i Digital Strategy samt vinder af Grand Prix-hovedprisen for årets digitale case.

**Jobbet**


Som D365 CE Funktionel Konsulent centrerer dine opgaver sig om videreudvikling og konfiguration af applikationerne og sikre sammenhængen mellem CE og Power flows. Derudover vil du yde løbende forretningssupport, tests, m.m., både selv og i samarbejde med kollegaer og eksterne partnere. Her er din forståelse for ’Out-of-the-Box’ applikationsfunktionalitet over mod forankring af nye løsninger i forretningen gennem dokumentation og træning vigtige succeskriterier. Arbejdsmetoden er agil og med brug af DevOps.
  

  

Dine opgaver i det daglige fokuserer på:

* Hovedansvar for udvalgte områder af CE-applikationen, hvor du sætter retning og også er hands-on.
* Gennem tæt dialog med forretningen sikre, at løsningsdesign afspejler behov og krav.
* Konfiguration – enten selv eller i samarbejde med MS-partnere.
* Sikre, at løsningerne leveres inden for tid, scope og lever op til de aftalte kvalitetskrav.
* I tæt samarbejde med en løsningsarkitekt sikre, at løsninger konfigureres iht. virksomhedens designprincipper
* Applikationstests af leverancer.
* Udarbejde dokumentation og træningsmateriale / supportere i brugen af løsningen.
* Udføre support.
* Holde dig opdateret på dit fagområde via uddannelse, seminarer og netværk.

**Profilen**


Vi byder dig velkommen, som er initiativrig og selvmotiverende, og hvor ansvarlighed, ordentlighed og ejerskab er nogle af dine vigtige værdier, og det gælder både i din omgang med forretningen, dine kollegaer og eksterne partnere. Hertil er du nysgerrig, empatisk og med et godt overblik, som gør dig i stand til at håndtere et komplekst IT-landskab og mange forskellige behov, fordi der er stor efterspørgsel fra forretningen. Egenskaber som proces og data-forståelse, systematik og analytisk sans mhp. at sikre fokus og fremdrift er også noget, som kendetegner dig.


Udover at være kunde- og løsningsorienteret kan du nikke genkendende til følgende faglige kvalifikationer:

* Erfaring indenfor D365 CE Sales eller Field Service.
* Komfortabel med at lave både drift og videreudvikling af applikationen.
* Indsigt i data og kundeprocesser mhp. løsningsdesign og kommunikation med forretningen.
* Flydende i dansk, skriftligt og mundtligt, og gerne et vist kendskab til engelsk.

**Afdelingen**


Du bliver del af et team af otte engagerede medspillere i Business Applications, som arbejder med D365 CE, S365 FO og Power-platformene samt andre applikationer. Med reference til Head of Business Applications får du en afvekslende hverdag samt faglig og personlig udvikling.


Der investeres heftigt i ny teknologi, som bliver brugt til at bidrage til Kemp & Lauritzens fortsatte vækst, og som også sikrer, at IT-organisationen kan være en værdifuld samarbejdspartner til forretning og kunder. Der kommer helt naturligt – og hele tiden – nye spændende opgaver ifm. ambitionerne om videreudvikling af digitalisering og AI. Udover dine direkte kollegaer sparrer du dagligt med i ca. 35 dygtige kollegaer (og stadigt voksende) i IT-organisationen samt næsten lige så mange eksterne konsulenter, som alle bidrager til at levere værdiskabende teknologi til ’core IT’ og digitale produkter til slutkunder.

**Kontaktinformation**


Hudson Nordic håndterer rekrutteringen i samarbejde med Kemp & Lauritzen, og du er velkommen til at kontakte Assignment Manager Mehmet Kökten på mail mehk@hudsonnordic.com eller på telefon 31249901 for mere information om stillingen.


Lyder dette som noget for dig? Så søg stillingen via ’ansøg’ ovenfor.

**Om Kemp & Lauritzen**


Kemp & Lauritzen A/S er Danmarks førende digitale og grønne teknikpartner med 3.000 medarbejdere fordelt over hele landet. Vi leverer løsninger inden for el, VVS, ventilation og køl – fra daglig service til store entrepriser og unikke specialer.


Hos Kemp & Lauritzen får du en hverdag fyldt med udfordringer, indflydelse og gode muligheder for at udvikle både faglige og personlige kompetencer. Der er høj arbejdsglæde, og det måles løbende og følges op, da der er et ønske om at være en af Danmarks bedste arbejdspladser. Det betyder, at der er fokus på udvikling, fællesskab og forskellighed, og en vigtig værdi i alle sammenhænge er ordentlighed.


Virksomheden er ejet af Axel Muusfeldts Fond, som investerer i deres medarbejdere og samfundet som helhed.

***Hos Kemp & Lauritzen handler det om ordentlighed***

title: Kubernetes & DevOps Architect
description:
### **Var med och forma modern plattformsarkitektur**


Som **Kubernetes & DevOps Arkitekt** blir du en del av **Cloud Platform Services** inom **Advania Managed Services**. Här får du en senior och rådgivande roll där du ansvarar för design, tekniska vägval och lösningsarkitektur inom Kubernetes, OpenShift, Linux, Open Source och hybrid cloud.


Rollen har ett tydligt ansvar för att omsätta kundens behov till hållbara, säkra och skalbara lösningar som fungerar i praktiken och passar Advanias leveransmodell. Du får möjlighet att påverka både kundlösningar, tekniska standarder, arbetssätt och hur vi utvecklar vårt erbjudande inom Kubernetes och DevOps området.

#### **Det här kommer du att göra**


Du arbetar nära kunder, specialister, arkitekter och interna funktioner inom Managed Services. Tyngdpunkten ligger i rådgivning, design och tekniska vägval, men med en tydlig förståelse för hur lösningarna ska implementeras, drivas och förvaltas över tid.


Du kommer bland annat att:

* designa plattformslösningar inom Kubernetes, OpenShift, containerteknik och hybrid cloud
* leda tekniska dialoger med kunder kring målbild, behov, vägval och lösningsförslag
* ta fram arkitektur, principer och rekommendationer för säkra, robusta och skalbara miljöer
* omsätta kundbehov till tekniska vägval och genomförbara lösningar
* bidra till utveckling av erbjudande, tjänsteinnehåll och leveransmodell
* stötta implementationsteam i tekniska beslut och prioriteringar
* bidra till förbättring av drifts, förvaltnings och standardiseringsrutiner

##### **Hur vi ser att du arbetar**


Vi söker dig som driver tekniska frågor framåt med hög grad av självständighet och helhetssyn. Du väger alternativ, beroenden och konsekvenser mot varandra, prioriterar inom givna ramar och tar ansvar för att lösningar håller hög kvalitet från design till förvaltning.


I kunddialogen skapar du förtroende genom att lyssna in behov, förenkla komplexitet och kommunicera tydligt kring rekommendationer och tekniska vägval. Du kan kombinera strategiskt perspektiv med praktisk förståelse och bidrar med riktning, kunskapsdelning och stöd till kollegor utan att behöva ha ett formellt personalansvar.

### **Vi söker dig som**


Vi söker dig som har **flerårig erfarenhet av infrastruktur i större eller tekniskt komplexa miljöe**r och som är trygg i att ta ansvar för design, rådgivning och tekniska vägval inom **Kubernetes**, **containerplattformar** och **modern plattformsarkitektur.** Du har stark kompetens inom **Linux** och **Open Source**, samt erfarenhet av **Red Hat** **miljöer** där **OpenShift** är ett tydligt prioriterat område.


Du har arbetat kund eller leveransnära och är van att omsätta tekniska behov till rekommendationer, arkitektur och lösningar som går att genomföra, driva och förvalta över tid. Du kombinerar teknisk höjd med helhetssyn, väger alternativ och konsekvenser mot varandra och skapar riktning i tekniska dialoger med kunder och interna intressenter.

##### **Det är särskilt viktigt att du har erfarenhet av:**

* Kubernetes och containerbaserade plattformar
* Linux och Open Source i praktiken
* design, rådgivning eller tekniska vägval inom plattform, container eller närliggande område
* kunddialog där tekniska behov omsatts till rekommendationer och lösningar
* DevOps i praktiken, till exempel automation, CI/CD, arbetssätt och leverans

##### **Det är meriterande om du har erfarenhet av:**

* OpenShift på djupet
* hybrid cloud arkitektur
* Ceph, Ansible, RHEL, Docker eller VMware Tanzu
* att bygga eller vidareutveckla tekniska erbjudanden
* certifieringar inom Kubernetes, Red Hat eller arkitektur och plattform


Du kommunicerar **obehindrat** på **svenska** och **engelska**, **både i tal och skrift.**

##### **Det här blir du en del av**


I Cloud Platform Services, en del av Advania Managed Services, arbetar du med modern plattformsarkitektur i komplexa och varierade IT miljöer. Tillsammans med erfarna kollegor bidrar du till stabila, säkra och skalbara leveranser där Kubernetes, OpenShift, Linux, Open Source och automation är centrala delar.


Här får du utvecklas i ett specialistteam där kunskapsdelning, teknisk riktning och ansvar nära kunden är en naturlig del av vardagen. Du får också möjlighet att påverka lösningar, förbättra arbetssätt och bidra till hur vi stärker vårt erbjudande och våra leveranser inom ett växande specialistområde.

**Placering**: Rollen utgår gärna från Stockholm, men vi är öppna för placering vid något av våra kontor, från Malmö i söder till Luleå i norr.

### **Vi ser fram emot din ansökan!**

Visa gärna ditt intresse så snart som möjligt då vi intervjuar löpande. Vi välkomnar sökanden med olika bakgrunder, erfarenheter och synsätt, eftersom vi vet att en bredare mångfald berikar var och en av oss och stärker såväl effektivitet som kreativitet i vårt arbete.


Som en del av vår rekryteringsprocess genomgår våra slutkandidater en obligatorisk bakgrundskontroll, en för oss kvalitativ kontroll inför ett anställningsbeslut. Advania samarbetar med Ljung & Sjöberg i ärenden kopplat till alkohol-, drog- och spelproblematik och är en certifierad "Proaktiv Arbetsplats".


Tveka inte att höra av dig om du har några frågor eller funderingar.️
company_description: Advania är ett techbolag som utformar skräddarsydda lösningar och skapar hållbara värden genom smart användning av teknik.

title: Kubernetes & DevOps Engineer
description:
### **Var med och utveckla modern plattformsdrift**


Som **Kubernetes & DevOps Engineer** blir du en del av **Cloud Platform Services** inom **Advania Managed Services.** Här arbetar du operativt med Kubernetes, Linux, Open Source och automation i komplexa miljöer där stabilitet, säkerhet och kvalitet är avgörande.


Rollen stärker vår leveranskapacitet i både kunduppdrag och interna tjänsteleveranser. Du implementerar, konfigurerar och vidareutvecklar containerbaserade plattformar, samtidigt som du bidrar till arbetssätt som gör leveranserna mer effektiva, säkra och skalbara. Det ger dig möjlighet att påverka tekniska lösningar, förbättra rutiner och bidra till hur vi utvecklar våra leveranser över tid.

#### **Det här kommer du att göra**


Du arbetar nära kunder, arkitekter, konsulter och specialister inom infrastruktur och plattform. Tyngdpunkten ligger i operativ specialistleverans där du tar ansvar för egna uppgifter och delområden.


Du kommer bland annat att:

* implementera och vidareutveckla lösningar baserade på Kubernetes och containerteknik
* arbeta operativt i Linux och Open Source baserade plattformar
* hantera tekniska förändringar, förbättringar och felsökning i drift och kundmiljöer
* bidra till automation och standardisering av återkommande arbeten
* delta i teknisk dialog med kunder och interna intressenter
* bidra till dokumentation, driftsättning och förvaltningsrutiner

##### **Hur vi ser att du arbetar**


Vi söker dig som är trygg i teknisk leverans och som driver ditt arbete framåt på ett strukturerat sätt. Du prioriterar, planerar och genomför dina uppgifter inom givna ramar, tar ansvar för kvaliteten i leveransen och bidrar till förbättringar i arbetssätt, rutiner och tekniska lösningar.


I kunddialogen skapar du förtroende genom att sätta dig in i tekniska behov, kommunicera tydligt och omsätta dessa till rekommendationer och fungerande lösningar. Du samarbetar nära kollegor och kunder, delar kunskap och söker stöd när det behövs för att bidra till både teamets gemensamma resultat och kundens långsiktiga värde.

### **Vi söker dig som**


Vi söker dig som har erfarenhet av infrastruktur i större eller tekniskt komplexa driftsmiljöer och som är trygg i operativ teknisk leverans inom **Linux**, **Open Source** och **plattformsdrift**. Du har arbetat med **Kubernetes** eller en närliggande containerplattform och vill fortsätta utvecklas inom **modern plattform, automation** och **DevOps**.


Du arbetar strukturerat i miljöer där stabilitet, säkerhet och kvalitet är viktiga. Du tar ansvar för dina uppgifter, samarbetar nära kollegor och kunder och bidrar till lösningar som fungerar i praktiken, både i drift och vidareutveckling.

##### **Det är särskilt viktigt att du har erfarenhet av:**

* Linux och Open Source i praktiken
* Kubernetes eller en närliggande containerplattform
* operativt tekniskt arbete i drift, plattform eller leveransmiljö
* arbete med säkerhet, tillgänglighet, prestanda och skalbarhet
* Red Hat miljöer, där OpenShift är särskilt relevant
* DevOps i praktiken, till exempel automation, CI/CD, samarbete och effektiva leveranser

##### **Det är meriterande om du har erfarenhet av:**

* OpenShift på djupet
* Ceph
* Ansible
* RHEL
* Docker
* VMware Tanzu
* hybrid cloud
* certifieringar inom Kubernetes, Red Hat eller närliggande områden


Du kommunicerar **obehindrat på svenska och engelska, både i tal** och **skrift.**


Du behöver inte ha djup kompetens inom hela teknikstacken från start. I rollen finns möjlighet att bredda dig inom plattformsområdet, fördjupa dig inom OpenShift eller närliggande plattformar och ta större ansvar i kunddialog, standardisering och förbättringsarbete över tid.

#### **Det här blir du en del av**


I Cloud Platform Services, en del av Advania Managed Services, arbetar du med modern plattformsdrift i komplexa och varierade IT miljöer. Tillsammans med erfarna kollegor bidrar du till stabila, säkra och effektiva leveranser där Kubernetes, Linux, Open Source och automation är centrala delar.


Här får du utvecklas i ett specialistteam där kunskapsdelning, teknisk utveckling och ansvar nära kunden är en naturlig del av vardagen. Du får också möjlighet att påverka lösningar, förbättra arbetssätt och bidra till hur vi utvecklar våra leveranser över tid.

**Placering**: Rollen utgår gärna från Stockholm, men vi är öppna för placering vid något av våra kontor, från Malmö i söder till Luleå i norr.

### **Vi ser fram emot din ansökan!**

Visa gärna ditt intresse så snart som möjligt då vi intervjuar löpande. Vi välkomnar sökanden med olika bakgrunder, erfarenheter och synsätt, eftersom vi vet att en bredare mångfald berikar var och en av oss och stärker såväl effektivitet som kreativitet i vårt arbete.


Som en del av vår rekryteringsprocess genomgår våra slutkandidater en obligatorisk bakgrundskontroll, en för oss kvalitativ kontroll inför ett anställningsbeslut. Advania samarbetar med Ljung & Sjöberg i ärenden kopplat till alkohol-, drog- och spelproblematik och är en certifierad "Proaktiv Arbetsplats".
