Som Senior AI & Automation Engineer arbetar du från vårt huvudkontor Gigachop i Rosersberg tillsammans med vårt utvecklingsteam. Du blir en nyckelperson i arbetet med att utveckla AI-drivna lösningar och automatiserade processer som används i hela verksamheten.
  
  

**Du kommer att:**  

Utveckla och implementera AI-drivna lösningar och interna verktyg
  
  

Bygga automationer som effektiviserar arbetsflöden och minskar manuellt arbete
  
  

Integrera system, API och externa tjänster
  
  

Arbeta med moderna AI-plattformar och språkmodeller
  
  

Utveckla interna system och digitala produkter tillsammans med teamet
  
  

Ta ansvar för hela utvecklingsprocessen – från idé till produktion
  
  

Samarbeta nära verksamheten för att identifiera områden där AI och automation kan skapa värde
  
  

Här får du möjlighet att vara med och forma hur AI används inom ett växande företag och omsätta idéer till lösningar som används på riktigt.
  
Vad kan du förvänta dig av oss?
  
Ett kompetent och engagerat utvecklingsteam med korta beslutsvägar
  
  

Stor frihet att påverka teknikval, arbetssätt och lösningar
  
  

Möjlighet att driva och utveckla ChopChops AI-initiativ
  
  

Ett företag i stark tillväxt med höga ambitioner
  
  

Ett modernt huvudkontor i Rosersberg där vi arbetar tillsammans på plats
  
  

En roll där du får kombinera utveckling, innovation och affärsnytta
  
  

**Vi tror att du:**  

Har flera års erfarenhet som Fullstack Developer, Backend Developer eller Software Engineer
  
  

Har stark kompetens inom systemutveckling och moderna webbteknologier
  
  

Har erfarenhet av Node.js, TypeScript, JavaScript eller liknande tekniker
  
  

Har arbetat med API, integrationer och databaser
  
  

Har erfarenhet av eller ett stort intresse för AI, LLM och automation
  
  

Är van att ta ägarskap och driva projekt från idé till färdig lösning
  
  

Är nyfiken, lösningsorienterad och gillar att lära dig ny teknik
  
  

Meriterande
  
Erfarenhet av OpenAI, Claude, Anthropic eller andra AI-plattformar
  
  

Erfarenhet av AI-agenter, RAG-lösningar eller workflow automation
  
  

Erfarenhet av AWS, Docker eller Kubernetes
  
  

Erfarenhet av verktyg som n8n, LangGraph, LangChain eller liknande
  
  

Tjänsten är placerad på vårt huvudkontor Gigachop i Rosersberg och kräver närvaro på plats.
  
**Lön:** Månadslön enligt överenskommelse.
  
Låter det intressant? Skicka in din ansökan och berätta hur du har arbetat med utveckling, AI och automation. Vi ser fram emot att höra från dig!

title: Senior R&D Engineer Advanced Electromagnetic Propulsion Systems
description:
Role Overview
  
We are seeking a highly skilled and visionary Senior R&D Engineer (Electromagnetic Propulsion) to lead the
  
physical modelling, simulation, and experimental validation of a next-generation, multistage Electromagnetic
  
Launch System.
  
In this role, you will bridge the gap between advanced physics theory and cutting-edge engineering. You will be
  
responsible for defining the mathematical framework of inductance acceleration, optimizing pulsed-power
  
efficiency, managing extreme thermal/mechanical stresses, and building physical prototypes to validate your
  
models. If you thrive on solving highly complex, multi-physics problems and want to shape the future of high-
  
velocity propulsion tech, this is your next challenge.
  
Core Responsibilities
  
1. Physics Modelling & Electromagnetic Analysis
  
* Develop the fundamental mathematical frameworks and multi-physics models (electromagnetic, thermal,


and material physics) to describe inductance acceleration and transient system behaviours.
  
* Investigate complex magnetic Lorentz forces, skin effects, and proximity effects acting on coils under


high-frequency, time-varying currents.
  
* Utilize advanced simulation tools to analyse a large parameter space (coil geometry, shape, size, and


turn count) to optimize magnetic field production and projectile acceleration.
  
2. Multistage System Architecture & Optimization
  
* Design and analyse multistage acceleration configurations, defining the optimal number of stages,


mutual stage interactions, and microsecond-level activation timing architectures.
  
* Optimize the stored-to-kinetic energy conversion efficiency by minimizing un-utilized magnetic flux,


ohmic/resistive losses, and eddy current heating.
  
* Define the boundary conditions, switching requirements, pulse shapes, and power regulation


dependencies for the electrical and control subsystems.
  
3. Component Design & System Integration
  
* Collaborate with external project partners to integrate custom-engineered, high-current pulsed-power


coils optimized for mechanical stability and thermal resilience.
  
* Oversee the concept design of specialized payloads to ensure seamless compatibility with the


electromagnetic launch environment.
  
* Address system-level integration challenges arising from high-intensity electromagnetic fields on


surrounding components.
  
4. Thermal Management & Power Control
  
* Identify transient heat sources within the accelerator stages and propose highly effective thermal


management and cooling solutions.
  
* Optimize the physical routing of the electrical power supply to the coil inductors to handle extreme stress


environments.
  
5. Experimental Validation & Safety
  
* Design, build, and operate an experimental test setup to validate simulation data, testing for acceleration


efficiency, discharge pulse dynamics, and heat management.
  
  

* Conduct comprehensive safety analyses on high-voltage and high-energy electromagnetic components.
* Establish strict safety protocols and ensure total compliance with relevant electrical safety standards for pulsed-power operations.


**Required Technical Skills & Experience:**  

* Education: Master’s or Ph.D. in Physics, Electrical Engineering, or a highly related field with a focus on


Electromagnetics.
  
* Simulation Mastery: Extensive experience with multi-physics simulation software (e.g., COMSOL,


Altair Flux, or MATLAB/Simulink) focusing on transient electromagnetic fields.
  
* Pulsed Power Expertise: Knowledge of inductance acceleration, magnetic forces, high-current


switching, and power electronics.
  
* Thermal & Mechanical Insight: Familiarity with managing thermal dissipation and mechanical stress in


high-energy electrical systems.
  
* Hands-on Testing: Experience setting up laboratory diagnostics, high-speed data acquisition systems,


and working safely with high-voltage hardware.
  
  

**Preferred Attributes:**  

* Experience in advanced aerospace or defence R&D, specifically in coil gun, or linear induction motor


technologies.
  
* Strong collaborative skills for working alongside external manufacturing and project partners.
* A meticulous approach to safety protocols and risk analysis.


What We Offer
  
* Cutting-Edge Projects: Work on a disruptive, first-of-its-kind electromagnetic technology program.
* Collaborative Culture: Join a highly collaborative environment alongside leading industry partners and


academic experts.
  
* Competitive Compensation: Attractive salary and continuous professional development paths.


Due to the nature of this project, applicants must be eligible to obtain the required national security clearances.
  
Öppen för alla
  
Vi fokuserar på din kompetens, inte dina övriga förutsättningar. Vi är öppna för att anpassa rollen eller arbetsplatsen efter dina behov.

title: ELECTRONIC ENGINEER POSITION CANDIDATE PROFILE
description:
Ytsab Defence AB specializes in advanced electromagnetic launch systems and high-
  
voltage pulsed power engineering. We are leading the development of next-generation
  
defence capabilities through innovative, modular architectures.
  
We are looking for a talented and driven Electronics Engineer to join our development team,
  
where you will play a critical role in the research, design, and optimization of the core
  
electromagnetic launch system. You will focus on the physics of multistage acceleration,
  
power regulation, bridging theoretical models with physical experimental setups. You will
  
work closely with international partners to integrate your designs with advanced switching
  
systems, capacitor banks, and thermal management architectures.
  
  

**As an Electronics Engineer at Ytsab Defence, you will:**  

* Design, test, and integrate advanced electronic systems for electromagnetic


weapons.
  
* Work closely with mechanical and software engineers to develop complete system


solutions.
  
* Develop PCBs, high-speed switching circuits, and power electronics.
* Support system integration, testing, and troubleshooting of electronic components.
* Contribute to R&D activities and new product development from concept to


prototype.
  
We’re Looking For
  
* A degree in Engineering Physics, Electrical or Electronics Engineering (BSc, MSc, or


equivalent).
  
* Solid experience in analogue and digital circuit design.
* Experience with power electronics, high current systems, or pulse power is a big


plus.
  
* PCB design experience (Altium Designer or similar).
* Familiarity with embedded systems or microcontrollers is advantageous.


Strong problem-solving skills and the ability to work independently in a fast-paced
  
R&D environment.
  
* Fluent in English; Swedish is a bonus.


Due to the nature of this project, applicants must be eligible to obtain the required national
  
security clearances.

title: Deep Learning Engineer
description:
Do you want to create technology for the next generation of industry and society? Univrses is now looking for a Deep Learning Engineer to join the team in Stockholm!


Univrses is a 3D Computer Vision and Machine Learning company based in Stockholm, creating high-end technologies for autonomous systems. We work in several different areas, but our primary focus is on self-driving vehicles, mobile robotics and smart city development. Our team consists of hard-working and friendly people from all over the world with diverse backgrounds and expert knowledge in computer vision, robotics, machine learning, physics, math, software development and more.

#### **Your role**


As our new Deep Learning Engineer, you will design and implement cutting-edge Computer Vision algorithms and pipelines for advanced perception systems. The work also includes deployment of such solutions, which includes tasks such as testing, deployment configuration and embedded integration, which can require writing C++ code. You will work in close collaboration with scientists and other engineers. Together with your team, you will develop technologies targeted for self-driving vehicles and advanced driver assistance systems that will have a major impact on how people live their lives. You will work in an agile team and collaborate closely with some of the best engineers and scientists in the field.

#### **What you bring**

* Solid experience with developing clean and production-ready code in Python.
* Experience with software testing (unit test, integration test, smoke test etc).
* A solid understanding of Deep Learning and Computer Vision.
* Experience in developing DL models, analyzing, and deploying them.
* Experience working with Agile software development principles.
* Experience in DL model deployment optimization (e.g., integration, pruning, quantization).
* Familiarity with CI/CD workflow
* Masters or equivalent in machine learning, computer science or a related field.
* Strong problem-solving skills and the ability to work in a collaborative team environment.
* Excellent communication and presentation skills in English.

#### **What we offer**

* Bright and spacious facilities in the heart of Södermalm, Stockholm
* Open and friendly work environment
* The best co-workers in the world – from all over the world!
* Generous employee benefits, such as:


	+ Flexible work hours
	+ 5 000 SEK/year in wellness grant
	+ Pension plan
* Weekly Lunch and Learns

#### **Next step**


Interested in joining Univrses as our new Deep Learning Engineer? Submit your application today - All application material must be in English!

title: Model Geometry Engineer
description:
**About Justera Group**  

Justera Group AB is a leading Swedish IT consulting firm with great experience specializing in building high-performing technology teams and delivering end-to-end IT solutions. Led by dynamic female executives, Justera Group focuses on helping businesses harness the power of digital technologies to continuously evolve in today’s rapidly changing economy. The company has deep expertise in IT consultancy, software development, and recruitment services, providing clients with purpose-driven, well-trained consultants who consistently deliver efficient and effective results. Justera Group manages the entire talent lifecycle, from hiring to ongoing management, ensuring the right fit for each project on time and within budget. With their strong commitment to quality, collaboration, and client success, Justera Group is a trusted partner for technology innovation across Sweden.  

  

**About the Role:**  

We are seeking an experienced Model Geometry Engineer to join a highly skilled design and development team responsible for the geometry creation and refinement of both interior and exterior automotive design models. The role involves supporting the development of physical and digital presentation models, including full-scale clay models, milled components, and 3D-printed parts used throughout the design and validation process.


This position requires close collaboration with designers, modelers, and cross-functional engineering teams to ensure high-quality geometry delivery that meets project requirements and timing.

**Key Responsibilities:**

* Develop and maintain geometry for interior and exterior design models.
* Create and modify presentation models, color and trim bucks, and show models.
* Prepare and optimize CAD data for milling, prototype construction, and 3D printing.
* Support the development of full-scale clay models and other physical design representations.
* Ensure geometry quality, manufacturability, and compliance with project standards.
* Collaborate with multidisciplinary and multicultural teams throughout the design process.
* Contribute to prototype and show vehicle development activities.

**Requirements:**  

5–10 years of experience working with **CATIA V5** within the automotive industry.

* Strong experience in prototype construction and geometry development using CATIA V5 or similar CAD platforms.
* Proven background in developing:


	+ Show cars and concept vehicles
	+ Milling models
	+ 3D printing models
	+ Presentation and design validation models
* Strong understanding of automotive design and model development processes.
* Fluent English communication skills, both written and verbal.

**What We Offer:**

* A dynamic and innovative workplace driving advancements in cutting-edge robotic technologies
* The opportunity to collaborate with talented cross-functional teams on meaningful and impactful projects
* Competitive compensation and comprehensive benefits package
* A supportive culture that fosters continuous learning, growth, and professional development
* Flexible hybrid working model for an optimal work-life balance
* 25 days of annual leave to recharge and relax
* An annual wellness allowance of 3,500 SEK to support your health and wellbeing

title: Test & Verification Engineer- Automotive
description:
**Test & Verification Engineer – SWIM Resolution Engineer**


Gothenburg | Start: 1 August 2026 | Apply as soon as possible

**About the Role**
------------------


We are looking for an experienced **SWIM Resolution Engineer** with a strong passion for automotive development and testing. You will work in a key role within software verification, collaborating closely with a team in China, and contributing to resolving technical issues in complex vehicle programs.


The role requires a structured and analytical mindset – you will identify, analyze, and resolve issues with high precision, ensuring that solutions are sustainable in the long term.

**Responsibilities**
--------------------

* Identify and analyze technical issues and their root causes in vehicle programs during testing
* Implement sustainable solutions and actively prevent recurring issues
* Evaluate, select, and verify solutions in close collaboration with product, operations, and engineering teams
* Contribute to the implementation of agreed verification plans and test cases
* Report test progress to the test leader and project team, and identify and escalate verification risks
* Work closely with the EE Center team in China
* Contribute to a culture of continuous improvement and participate in initiatives to increase efficiency

**Qualifications**
------------------

* Bachelor’s degree in Electrical Engineering, Computer Engineering, or similar
* Minimum 4 years of experience in embedded systems development within the automotive industry
* Experience in testing and verification of automotive systems
* Proficiency with Vector tools such as CANoe or CANalyzer
* Strong analytical skills and experience in test analysis
* Cultural understanding of China–Europe collaboration
* Valid Swedish driver’s license
* **Required:** Fluent Mandarin (spoken and written) and strong English skills (spoken and written)

**Personal Attributes**
-----------------------

* Genuine interest in cars and experience with vehicle testing
* Strong communication skills
* High collaboration ability and comfortable working in international teams

**Other Information**
---------------------

* Travel may be required for expeditions and workshops
* Workload: 100%
* Location: Gothenburg (100% onsite)

**Apply**
---------


Submit your application no later than **9 July 2026**. We review applications continuously, so do not wait to apply.


We look forward to hearing from you!

title: Technical Engineering Manager
description:
Auctionet is a marketplace where auction houses from Sweden, Great Britain, Finland, Spain, Germany, and Denmark sell furniture, art, design objects, collectibles, and much more. We are now looking for a **Technical Engineering Manager** who wants to be part of our team and contribute to a fast, reliable marketplace used by over a million buyers.

**About the Role**
------------------


We’re hiring our **second Engineering Manager** for one of our product teams.


This is a hands-on leadership role. You’re responsible for the developers’ growth and performance, and for the team’s ability to deliver well — continuously, and at a sustainable pace. With just one team to look after, you’ll also work alongside the team on projects, contributing to product work yourself.


You’ll shape how this works together with the team and the others who lead with you — the Tech Lead on technical direction and feasibility, and Product Managers and Designers in their areas.

**Your responsibilities will include:**

* **Managing the team’s four developers and one designer** — 1:1s, feedback, growth, performance management, hiring, and onboarding.
* **Helping the team deliver well** — continuously and sustainably, working with Product Managers and Designers on planning, prioritisation, and unblocking.
* **Improving how the team works** — ways of working and team building.
* **Raising engineering standards** — testing, CI/CD, observability, and incident response.
* **Working hands-on with the team on projects** — building features alongside the developers, reviewing code, and pairing where it helps.


This role is based in **Stockholm**, hybrid (partly remote possible). Working language is **English**, and we’re looking to start **from August** — flexible on the exact date for the right person.

**About the Team**
------------------


You’ll be the Engineering Manager for our **Auctionet Backoffice Team** — a six-person product team working mainly on internal tooling and products. Your people-management responsibility is for the team’s **four developers**.


The team builds in Ruby on Rails, with Turbo, Stimulus, and PostgreSQL — no need to already know it.


Auctionet runs the leading online marketplace for European auction houses — art, design, furniture, and antiques. We have around 170 employees and are the parent company of a wider group with roughly 400 colleagues across multiple countries. Our main office is near Stureplan in central Stockholm, with logistics centres in Malmö, Stockholm, Gothenburg, Westbury, and Köln.

**About You**
-------------


You’re pragmatic, you communicate clearly, and you help the team keep moving.

**To succeed in this role, we think you have:**

* **Experience managing software engineers** — not only as a project or tech lead, and you’ve helped individuals and teams grow.
* **Still hands-on as an engineer** — happy to pick up real work and ship it: coding, code review, testing, monitoring, and security basics.
* **Used to shipping user-facing product** in small iterations.
* **Experience helping teams through change** — bringing people with you.
* **Good at working with others** — your team, other teams, Product Managers, Designers, Tech Leads, and stakeholders across Auctionet; comfortable in English (Swedish is a plus).

**Bonus and not mandatory:**

* Interest in art, design, or collectibles.

**Benefits**
------------

* **30 days' paid vacation.**
* **Flexible holiday swap** — trade two public holidays for ones you choose.
* **8% occupational pension** — on top of your salary.
* **Parental pay** — a top-up to your paid parental leave.
* **Wellness** — a 4,000 SEK yearly grant, plus one paid hour a week during working hours.
* **Health support** — counselling, rehabilitation, and income support during long-term sick leave.
* **Learning & development** — an annual budget for courses, conferences, and professional growth.
* **Art Club** — events, exhibitions, and openings through our staff association.


... and a few more perks beyond these.

**How we hire**
---------------


We see hiring as a two-way thing. We’re mindful of your time, but we also want to spend enough of it together for both of us to get a real feel for working as a team — a short intro call, then two or three conversations with the team, and a small take-home exercise you can do in your own time. No whiteboard puzzles — we’d rather talk through real work together.

**Apply By**
------------


We will be reviewing applications continuously.


If you’re excited about this role but don’t tick every box for the role, we’d still love to hear from you. Very few people match every point — so if you’re close and motivated, please apply.

**Who We Are**
--------------


Auctionet was founded in 2011 by a team of people with unique experience of online auctions. Read more about Auctionet here.


In 2021 Auctionet acquired the auction house Stockholms Auktionsverk, and we are now by far the largest auction marketplace in the Nordics, and one of the larger auction marketplaces in the world. With this acquisition, Auctionet owns and operates both the oldest auction house in the world (1674) and the second oldest (1681), which means we have been working with sustainability for 350(!) years.


Join us in our vision to make valued objects accessible for everyone, from everywhere!

title: Product Engineer
description:
**Aplex Bio** is at the forefront of advanced molecular testing, delivering platform technologies that expand capabilities in precision genomics, molecular diagnostics, and high-frequency biological screening. Hyperplex PCR, the company’s next-generation platform, introduces a new paradigm in molecular measurement by combining multiplexed detection, molecular counting, and single-nucleotide discrimination in a single workflow. The platform delivers digital-like single-copy sensitivity while scaling to 100+ targets per well, addressing limitations in plex, precision, and cost-efficiency that constrain both traditional PCR and sequencing technologies. Aplex Bio is dedicated to advancing the next generation of molecular diagnostics and enabling technologies for population-scale applications that can contribute to improved healthcare outcomes worldwide. The company is headquartered in Stockholm, Sweden.


**Tasks**
---------



We are seeking a scientist to (1) support the development and optimization of customized Hyperplex PCR panels spanning a variety of high impact biological applications and (2) support ongoing R&D developments of new product lines. The successful candidate will report directly to the Chief Scientific Officer and be involved in the improvement and optimization and validation workflows; hands-on experimentation; systematic critical data analysis and documentation, as well as operational logistics of procuring reagents and assembly of the final kit product. The position is ideal for an action-driven problem solver who thrives in an interdisciplinary, fast-paced start-up environment. We are seeking someone with exceptional attention to detail, meticulous organizational skills, and a strong commitment to precise documentation. Your ability to follow data documentation guidelines to the letter, and maintain an organized, detail-oriented approach in all tasks, will be critical in ensuring success. If you pride yourself on your attention to the smallest details and have a passion for keeping things structured and well-documented, this is the perfect role for you.


**Responsibilities**


* Collaborate closely with the biology team on the performance quality control of primers and probes for specific DNA and RNA targets of interest
* Drive and continuously improve routines of oligo procurement, storage and handling to support multiple parallel custom panel development projects
* Develop efficient optimization and validation workflows according to internal standards and external customer requests
* Support the implementation and expansion of liquid handling automation tools for streamlining production and routine assay execution
* Hands-on preparation of oligo mixes and experimentation aimed at the optimization and validation of new hpPCR panels, as well as ongoing product development projects
* Plan adaptation and scaling of operations to cope with expanding volumes of customization requests
* Compile and analyse large data sets and prepare clear reporting documents for internal and external communication purposes
* Continuously support an efficient communication with the chemistry and software teams for quality assurance and improvement of internal processes
* Routine interactions with new and existing customer-base to provide scientific support, bespoke development of panels according to needs of customers and troubleshooting according to customer feedback
* Keeping up to date with relevant scientific and technical developments

**Requirements**
----------------



We’re looking for a candidate with a strategic mindset, action-driven approach, and the ability to thrive in a fast-paced and agile environment. This individual should possess meticulous attention to detail and a proactive approach to achieving project milestones, as well as the flexibility to adapt to evolving methods. The ideal candidate can communicate effectively at all organizational levels, build strong relationships with team members and existing customers, and demonstrate a genuine passion for making a disruptive impact in molecular diagnostics.


*Required:*


* MSc or PhD degree in Biotechnology, Bioengineering, Biomedicine, Microbiology or related fields
* Experience running and optimizing qPCR and/or digital PCR assays
* Basic knowledge of statistical analysis, including data classification, regression, clustering and visualization
* Strong laboratory skills and hands-on approach
* Excellent ability to characterize and navigate complex problems to find solutions
* Strong written and verbal communication skills in English.
* Valid work authorization for Sweden


*Preferred:*


* At least 2 years of industry experience
* Experience with bioinformatic tools for primer design, sequence alignment and basic programming tools
* Experience in Library preparation for Next-generation sequencing
* Experience in Fluorescence Microscopy
* Experience in lab management and Laboratory Information Management Systems (LIMS)
* Experience with multiplexed detection of biomarkers (using methods and systems such as Luminex, Nanostring, Olink etc) and/or multiplex PCR
* Experience working with padlock probes and rolling circle amplification

**Benefits**
------------


**What we offer**


* An ambitious, innovative, inclusive and professional environment.
* Being part of building a cross-disciplinary technology at the frontier of its space.
* Nordic level salary with 5 weeks annual paid vacation.
* Stock option program.


We are hiring on a rolling basis. You will be a key part of our scale-up journey and will be joining a high-paced energetic and diverse team.
